# WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0007 - QUIC RFC 9001 Known Stateless Reset Loop Suppression Work Item

## Summary

Implement and prove the retained-route known-token loop-suppression matrix cell after AEAD-limit terminal discard.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0008

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0007

## Planned Changes

- Add an explicit non-emitted disposition for retained-route triggers that are known stateless resets.
- Suppress emission before rate-budget reservation when a reset-shaped retained-route trigger carries the retained token for the same remote address.
- Keep different trailing tokens eligible for the existing retained-route response path when the route, remote endpoint, loop-prevention, and rate gates pass.
- Add focused requirement-home coverage for direct endpoint suppression, different-token retained-route emission, non-reset-shaped exclusion, endpoint-host suppression, and deterministic fuzz coverage over route, remote endpoint, and token-match combinations.
- Add a permanent stateless-reset benchmark case for the known-reset suppression branch.

## Out of Scope

- Non-retained stateless-reset response behavior
- A new ambiguity-detection route model
- Token generation or token-storage changes
- Active-connection stateless-reset acceptance changes
- Key-update lifecycle broadening

## Verification Plan

Run the focused REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0008 requirement-home tests, keep the retained-route and formatting matrix guards green, refresh QuicStatelessResetBenchmarks in Dry mode, render and check affected SpecTrace Markdown from JSON, and finish with git diff --check.

## Completion Notes

Completed for the known-token loop-suppression cell only. Broader non-retained and untraced post-stop response behavior remains blocked in REQUIREMENT-GAPS.
